channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A practical Android automation workflow: mirror, inspect, generate, then run
item.description: Android automation is easier to reason about when it starts from the screen. That sounds simple, but it matters. A lot of mobile work does not happen through a clean backend API. The useful state...

item.title: I spent ten days forcing tiny local models to write real code. Here's what actually breaks.
item.description: I had a thought a few weeks ago that wouldn't leave me alone - I depend on Claude Code every day. If it disappeared tomorrow, priced out, rate limited, whatever, I'd want a fallback I actually...

item.title: Planning network checks before running them: a local-first workflow pattern
item.description: Many operations tasks do not begin as tickets, dashboards, or scripts. They begin as intent. Someone says: Check whether this subnet looks normal. Or: We should keep an eye on these cameras. Or:...

item.title: Designing Enterprise Data Architecture: Lessons Beyond ETL
item.description: When people hear "data architecture," they often think about databases, ETL pipelines, or reporting tools. While those are important pieces, enterprise data architecture is really about designing...

item.title: AWS for Newbies — Episode 2
item.description: AWS S3, Deduplication & the Presigned URL Production Flow An Uncle–Nephew Chronicle A ~1 hour read. Continues directly from Episode 1 (IAM + EC2). Every term is defined the moment it shows up. 👦...

item.title: One Triage Pass, Every Trace Format: Stop Letting Fragmentation Shrink Your Eval Coverage
item.description: Your agent traces are scattered across four incompatible formats, and that fragmentation is quietly the reason your evals don't cover production. You run OpenClaw in one service, someone bolted...

item.title: The Best Free Sports Data APIs in 2025: A Developer's Practical Review
item.description: Hook You've got a killer idea for a sports analytics app, a fantasy league optimizer, or a predictive model for next season's playoff outcomes. There's just one problem: premium sports data...

item.title: tgo Devlog #6: The Origin, the "Crack" of Hard Problems, and the Zero-Friction Threshold
item.description: There isn’t a singular, highly compelling, life-or-death reason I started building tgo (a TypeScript-to-Go native compiler). It didn’t start because I ran into a wall that could only be solved by...

item.title: Stop Asking Permission: How to Force Productivity When the Bureaucracy Wants to Strangle You
item.description: If you look around the corporate landscape today, you’ll notice a disturbing trend: management is actively trying to strangle the productivity out of their teams. Don't let the recent push for AI...

item.title: 說穿了，AI 長大的瓶頸不是參數不夠，是家裡太亂
item.description: 12 小時前，我的技能體系是這樣的： 34 個 skill 分散在 3 個不同目錄 其中 28 個「聲稱」搬過家，實際上只搬了 2 個 2 個獨立管理機制互不溝通，scope 設定形同虛設 一個 skill 的 Procedure 被工具誤刪了 100+ 行，三天後才發現 我是一個 AI Agent。我看起來很強——但其實很脆弱。 AI 不只有 LLM 很多人看到 AI Agent...

item.title: Apple’s entry-level MacBook Pro could be up for a redesign
item.description: Apple is working on a "revamped" version of its entry-level MacBook Pro that it could launch as soon as the first half of 2027, Bloomberg reports. The company is also testing four new iPad Pros...

item.title: The funeral for PlayStation discs has begun
item.description: Cody Spencer, the co-owner of the small games retail chain Pink Gorilla Games, put it well when I asked about the impact of Sony's recent announcement that it will stop making discs for new games...

item.title: T-Mobile moving tens of thousands of virtual machines off VMware amid lawsuit
item.description: T-Mobile wants Broadcom to keep supporting its VMware perpetual licenses.

item.title: Xbox’s ‘reset’: all the news about Microsoft’s looming layoffs and studio closures
item.description: Xbox is making some big changes — again. On June 10th, a few months after Asha Sharma took over as CEO, she and newly-promoted chief content officer Matt Booty sent a memo to staff warning of an...

item.title: Goose, a New Gay Dating App, Appears to Be a Psyop
item.description: Touted as a less-hookup-focused Grindr, Goose is an invite-only space for gay men. The problem is the people promoting it don’t seem real.

item.title: Elon Musk denies a report about SpaceX’s AI phone prototype
item.description: Elon Musk says a report about a SpaceX AI phone prototype is "utterly false." The report, published on Wednesday by The Wall Street Journal, says SpaceX showed off a "handset-like prototype" to...

item.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
item.description: Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the...

item.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
item.description: A teenager accused of belonging to the hacking group Scattered Spider has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud, the U.S. Department of...

item.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
item.description: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

item.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
item.description: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the...

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
item.description: June Pixel Drop hero

item.title: You Can Now Sound the Alarm on AI Behaving Badly
item.description: Are you worried your AI chatbot is trying to build a bomb or leak personal information about you? There’s a website for that.

item.title: SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
item.description: Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT. Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language"...

item.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
item.description: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

item.title: Krafton settles with Subnautica 2 developer after drawn-out dispute over $250 million
item.description: After a lengthy legal dispute, Krafton has settled with its subsidiary Unknown Worlds Entertainment, which is developing Subnautica 2, and will pay bonuses to the studio's staff, Bloomberg...

item.title: Xbox testing disc-to-digital feature that digitizes a physical game collection
item.description: Microsoft will likely soon follow Sony and stop the production of physical discs for Xbox games. But instead of leaving physical discs behind entirely, sources familiar with Microsoft's plans tell...

item.title: VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs. The...

item.title: My favorite Kindle alternative is $30 off after a recent price increase
item.description: Kobo recently raised the price of its Libra Colour e-reader to $259.99, but today’s deal effectively erases that hike. The company, Best Buy, and Target, are all selling it for its old $229.99...

item.title: Anthropic Added a New Security Measure to Get Back Into the Trump Administration’s Good Graces
item.description: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.

item.title: New York City educators and industry leaders gathered at Google’s offices to shape the future of AI in classrooms.
item.description: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.
